docs.title: Bedrock Health Group Governance & Compliance Policy Library
docs.text:
This is the controlled governance and compliance policy library for Bedrock Health Group.

This library is intended to support structured policy management, document control, review workflow, version history, and future policy access.

docs.title: Library Purpose
docs.text: The purpose of this library is to provide a centralized location for approved policies, templates, and policy governance materials.

docs.title: Current Status
docs.text: This library is maintained through Bedrock Health Group's controlled GitHub review and publication workflow. Policies listed in the Policy Index reflect the current public policy library records.

docs.title: Library Areas
docs.text:
• Compliance Program
  • Healthcare Compliance
  • Privacy & Security
  • Governance
  • Operations
  • Finance & Administration
  • Human Resources
  • Vendor Management
  • Training & Acknowledgements

docs.title: Document Control Principle
docs.text: Policies should be drafted, reviewed, approved, published, revised, and retired through a controlled process.

docs.title: Policy Index
docs.text:
The following policies have been added to the Bedrock Health Group Governance & Compliance Policy Library.

Policy ID Policy Name Category Status Effective Date Owner gov-byl-v1.2 Conflict of Interest Policy Governance Adopted Not specified in source document Governance & Compliance BHG-PRIV-001 Privacy Policy Privacy & Security Published Not specified in source website Privacy Office BHG-PRIV-002 Cookies Policy Privacy & Security Published Not specified in source website Privacy Office BHG-SEC-001 Email Security and Phishing Response Policy Privacy & Security Published 2026-04-25 Privacy & Security

docs.title: Status Definitions
docs.text: Status Meaning Draft Policy is being prepared and is not approved for operational use. Adopted Policy has been formally adopted by the appropriate governing authority. Published Policy has been published to the Bedrock Health Group policy library. Approved Policy has completed required review and approval. Under Review Policy is being reviewed or revised. Retired Policy is no longer active.

docs.title: How to Use This Library
docs.text:
The Bedrock Health Group Governance & Compliance Policy Library is the controlled public policy library for Bedrock Health Group.

This library contains public-facing governance, privacy, security, and compliance policy records that have been prepared, reviewed, approved, and published through Bedrock Health Group's controlled GitHub review and publication workflow.

docs.title: Official Source of Record
docs.text:
The live policy page in this library is the official controlled record for public Bedrock Health Group policies published here.

Printed, downloaded, or PDF copies should be verified against the live policy library before being relied upon.

docs.title: Public vs. Internal Policies
docs.text:
This public policy library is intended for policies and governance records that may be shared publicly.

Internal-only policies, procedures, playbooks, administrative instructions, system details, investigation procedures, and sensitive operational materials should not be published in this public library.

Internal-only materials should be maintained in a separate private/internal policy environment.

docs.title: How Policies Are Published
docs.text:
Public policies are published through the following workflow:

  1. A policy owner requests or drafts a policy.
  2. The policy is classified as public or internal.
  3. The policy is formatted using the Bedrock policy template.
  4. The Policy Index and site navigation are updated when needed.
  5. A GitHub pull request is opened.
  6. Required reviewers review the policy and publication readiness.
  7. The pull request is approved and merged.
  8. GitHub Actions publishes the updated policy library.
  9. The live policy URL becomes the controlled public record.

docs.title: Printed and PDF Copies
docs.text: Printed or PDF copies generated from the live policy library are convenience copies. Users should verify the current version, status, and effective date against the live policy page before relying on a printed or downloaded copy.

docs.title: Questions or Corrections
docs.text: Questions, corrections, or policy update requests should be submitted through the approved policy request or revision process.

docs.title: Policy Lifecycle
docs.text: Bedrock Health Group policies should move through a controlled lifecycle from request through publication, review, revision, and retirement.

docs.title: Lifecycle Stages
docs.text: Stage Meaning Draft Policy content is being prepared and is not approved for operational use. Under Review Policy content is being reviewed by the appropriate owner, reviewer, or approving authority. Published Policy has been approved for publication in the Bedrock Health Group policy library. Adopted Policy has been formally adopted by the appropriate governing authority, when required. Retired Policy is no longer active and should not be used for current operations.

docs.title: Standard Workflow
docs.text:
1. Policy request is submitted.
  2. Policy owner is assigned.
  3. Public or internal classification is confirmed.
  4. Draft is created using the Bedrock policy template.
  5. Required reviewers are identified.
  6. Policy is reviewed for substance, formatting, sensitivity, and publication readiness.
  7. Pull request is opened in GitHub.
  8. Required approval is obtained.
  9. Policy is merged and published.
  10. Policy is reviewed according to its review cycle.

docs.title: Review Cycle
docs.text:
Unless another review cycle is specified, Bedrock Health Group policies should be reviewed annually.

Each policy should include:

  • Policy ID
  • Version
  • Status
  • Effective date
  • Last reviewed date
  • Next review due date
  • Policy owner
  • Approving authority

docs.title: Public/Internal Classification
docs.text:
Before publication, every policy must be classified as either public-facing or internal-only.

Public-facing policies may be published in this public library.

Internal-only policies should be maintained in a separate private/internal policy environment.

docs.title: Retirement
docs.text: Retired policies should not be deleted without review. Where appropriate, they should be marked as retired, removed from public navigation, and retained in version history or internal records.

docs.title: Document Control
docs.text: Field Detail Policy Title Conflict of Interest Policy Policy ID / P&P # gov-byl-v1.2 Version 1.2 Status Adopted Policy Area Governance & Compliance Approval Date Adopted by the Board of Trustees on August 5, 2025 Effective Date Not specified in source document Review Cycle Annual Policy Owner Governance & Compliance Approved By Board of Trustees Confidentiality Level Publicly Accessible Governance Record

docs.title: Organization Information
docs.text: Field Detail Legal Name Bedrock Health Group State of Incorporation Louisiana Louisiana Charter Number 46599519N Incorporation Date August 4, 2025 EIN 39-3546068 Unique Entity ID P2DAPYN6BCV4 Primary Office Address 201 Saint Charles Ave, Ste 114 #788, New Orleans, LA 70170 Registered Agent Office 201 Rue Beauregard, STE 202, Lafayette, LA 70508

docs.title: 1. Purpose
docs.text:
Bedrock Health Group (“BHGroup”) is a charitable, tax-exempt organization whose credibility, community trust, and IRS standing depend on strict adherence to ethical standards. Because BHGroup operates within healthcare compliance, public health systems, safety-net ecosystems, and federally regulated environments, the potential for perceived or actual conflicts must be handled with exceptional rigor.

The purpose of this Conflict of Interest Policy is to ensure that all individuals entrusted with governance, review authority, advisory input, decision-making power, or operational influence conduct themselves in a manner that protects BHGroup from private benefit, undue influence, and jeopardy to its exempt status.

This policy establishes the standards, expectations, processes, and safeguards that govern how conflicts are identified, disclosed, evaluated, and managed. It applies to all individuals serving in Bedrock’s governance ecosystem, including the Board of Trustees, Board Officers, Advisory Board, Advisory Review Council (ARC), Community Funding Authorization Committee (CFAC), Governance, Ethics & Compliance Committee (GECC), and executive leadership.

This policy is designed to ensure that decisions are made solely in furtherance of BHGroup’s mission, free from personal bias or competing interests, and consistent with federal tax law, state nonprofit law, and Bedrock’s internal compliance framework.

docs.title: 2. Scope and Applicability
docs.text:
This policy applies to every individual who participates in governance or decision-making at Bedrock Health Group. This includes Trustees, officers, advisory members, committee members, key employees, and any contractor or subject-matter expert whose role grants access to nonpublic information, influence over decisions, or involvement in program evaluation, funding recommendations, or policy oversight.

Because Bedrock engages with vulnerable providers, federal systems, and regulated entities, even the appearance of a conflict can undermine trust. Individuals covered by this policy are required to uphold the highest standards of independence and ethical conduct, regardless of whether their role includes voting authority, advisory input, or evaluative responsibility.

docs.title: 3. Definitions
docs.text:
A conflict of interest exists whenever an individual’s personal, professional, financial, or relational interest could compromise their ability to act solely in the best interest of BHGroup. Conflicts may be actual, potential, or perceived.

An Interested Person is any individual subject to this policy who holds a role that influences BHGroup decisions.

An interested person may have a Financial Interest if they or a family/household member holds an ownership stake, investment interest, employment relationship, compensation arrangement, or prospective benefit involving an entity that interacts with BHGroup.

Interests may also be personal or professional, arising from advisory roles, volunteer positions, partnerships, alliances, or close relationships with applicants or stakeholders.

A Family Member includes spouses, domestic partners, parents, siblings, children, and individuals residing in the same household.

A financial or personal interest does not automatically constitute a conflict. The determination of whether a conflict exists is made through the evaluation process defined in this policy.

docs.title: 4. Duty to Disclose
docs.text:
All individuals covered by this policy have an affirmative obligation to disclose any relationship, interest, or circumstance that could impair or appear to impair their neutrality. Disclosure is required as soon as the individual becomes aware of the issue, regardless of whether they believe a conflict exists.

Full, timely disclosure is essential. Individuals must provide sufficient detail to enable an accurate evaluation of the situation.

GECC receives and evaluates disclosures for advisory bodies and committees. The Board of Trustees evaluates disclosures involving Trustees, officers, or GECC members.

Failure to disclose is itself considered a breach of fiduciary duty.

docs.title: 5. Recusal Requirements
docs.text:
Once a conflict is disclosed, the individual must immediately recuse themselves from all involvement in the matter, including discussions, scoring activities, deliberations, electronic workspaces, and communications.

Recusal applies across every governance body, including:

  • ARC members, who must not score or review applications involving any conflicting interest.
  • CFAC members, who must not vote on or influence funding decisions where a conflict exists.
  • Advisory Board members, who must not provide input on matters affecting conflicted entities or parties.
  • GECC members, who must not participate in conflict determinations involving themselves.
  • Trustees, who must not vote on or deliberate in matters involving their own interests or those of close associates.

An individual may not attempt to influence deliberations directly or indirectly once recused.

docs.title: 6. Evaluation of Conflicts
docs.text:
GECC evaluates conflicts involving advisory bodies, ARC, CFAC, staff, and subject-matter experts. The Board of Trustees evaluates conflicts involving GECC members or Trustees.

Conflict determinations consider whether the interest:

  • Could reasonably affect independent judgment.
  • Presents a risk of bias or undue influence.
  • Creates the appearance of impropriety.
  • Intersects with financial or organizational benefit.
  • Could compromise BHGroup’s compliance integrity.
  • Could undermine fairness in the Cornerstone Access Fund review process.
  • Conflicts with IRS private benefit restrictions.

The absence of financial benefit does not eliminate the possibility of a conflict.

docs.title: 7. Management of Conflicts
docs.text:
If GECC or the Board determines that a conflict exists, several measures may be implemented:

  1. Full recusal of the individual from all related matters.
  2. Assignment of an alternate reviewer, evaluator, or committee member.
  3. Restrictions on access to documentation or data related to the matter.
  4. Enhanced oversight of independently reviewed materials.
  5. Modification of committee structure for that funding cycle if needed to maintain independence.
  6. Engagement of external advisors when independence must be fortified.
  7. Board review of committee decisions for potential influence.

Where conflicts impact ARC or CFAC processes, GECC may require corrective action, including re-evaluation of scores, re-review of applications, or procedural adjustments.

docs.title: 8. Documentation and Recordkeeping
docs.text:
All disclosures, recusals, reviews, determinations, and conflict-related actions must be fully documented.

Meeting minutes must clearly record:

  • The nature of the conflict.
  • The disclosure made.
  • Individuals recused.
  • Determinations by GECC or the Board.
  • Alternatives considered.
  • Final decisions and rationale.
  • Votes cast by disinterested members.

GECC maintains a permanent ledger of conflict disclosures, recusal logs, annual certifications, and conflict-related audit trails.

docs.title: 9. Compensation and Related-Party Transactions
docs.text:
No individual may participate in or vote on their own compensation or on compensation involving related persons. All compensation decisions must be guided by independent comparability data and approved by disinterested Trustees.

Any transaction involving an entity connected to an interested person is subject to heightened scrutiny, must demonstrably further BHGroup’s charitable purposes, and must not result in private inurement or excessive benefit.

docs.title: 10. Annual Certification
docs.text:
All Trustees, officers, advisory members, ARC and CFAC participants, GECC members, and key individuals must complete an annual Conflict of Interest Disclosure Statement affirming that they:

  • Have received and reviewed this policy.
  • Understand their obligations.
  • Will promptly disclose conflicts.
  • Will comply with recusal procedures.
  • Recognize BHGroup’s 501(c)(3) responsibilities.

Annual certification is a condition of continued service.

docs.title: 11. Periodic Reviews
docs.text:
GECC shall conduct periodic evaluations of conflict-of-interest compliance, including:

  • Review of ARC and CFAC cycles for conflict integrity.
  • Verification that disclosures were properly handled.
  • Review of related-party transactions.
  • Examination of compensation decisions.
  • Assessment of governance structures for ongoing independence.

Findings may be presented to the Board for corrective action or policy refinement.

docs.title: 12. Use of Outside Experts
docs.text:
When necessary, BHGroup may engage independent legal counsel, auditors, or compliance professionals to assist in conflict determinations, compensation analysis, policy review, or governance oversight.

Use of experts supports but does not replace the Board’s fiduciary responsibility.

docs.title: Approval and Publication Record
docs.text:
Approval evidence, review history, and publication history are maintained through Bedrock Health Group's controlled GitHub review workflow, board records, and applicable acknowledgement records.

This policy page does not serve as an individual signature or acknowledgement form.

Where individual acknowledgement is required, Bedrock Health Group may maintain separate acknowledgement records through a designated acknowledgement process.

Field Detail Approval Method Board adoption and GitHub-controlled publication workflow Approving Authority Board of Trustees Approval Evidence Location Board records and GitHub pull request / commit history Publication Method MkDocs / GitHub Pages deployment Publication Trigger Merge to main branch Revision Tracking Git commit history and policy revision history

docs.title: Document Control
docs.text: Field Detail Policy Title Cookies Policy Policy ID BHG-PRIV-002 Version 1.0 Status Published Policy Area Privacy & Security Effective Date Not specified in source website Review Cycle Annual Policy Owner Privacy Office Approved By Governance & Compliance Confidentiality Level Publicly Accessible Policy Source https://bedrock-health-group.webdemotesting.online/policies/cookies/

docs.title: 1. Introduction
docs.text:
This Cookies Policy explains how Bedrock Health Group uses cookies and similar tracking technologies on its websites, portals, and digital services.

Cookies help support website functionality, security, analytics, user experience, and service improvement.

docs.title: 2. What Cookies Are
docs.text:
Cookies are small text files placed on a user’s device when visiting a website. Cookies may help a website recognize a user’s device, remember preferences, support secure sessions, or collect usage information.

Similar technologies may include pixels, tags, local storage, session storage, and analytics tools.

docs.title: 3. Types of Cookies We May Use
docs.text:
Bedrock Health Group may use the following types of cookies:

  • Strictly necessary cookies: Required for core website functionality, security, navigation, and access to protected areas.
  • Performance and analytics cookies: Help us understand how users interact with our website and services.
  • Functionality cookies: Remember preferences and improve user experience.
  • Security cookies: Help detect, prevent, and respond to security risks.
  • Third-party cookies: May be used by service providers that support website hosting, analytics, embedded content, or digital tools.

docs.title: 4. Why We Use Cookies
docs.text:
We may use cookies and related technologies to:

  • Operate and secure our website.
  • Support login and session management.
  • Understand website traffic and usage patterns.
  • Improve performance and user experience.
  • Maintain website reliability.
  • Support compliance, security, and fraud-prevention activities.

docs.title: 5. Managing Cookies
docs.text:
Users may manage cookies through their browser settings. Most browsers allow users to block, delete, or limit cookies.

Disabling certain cookies may affect website functionality, login features, or user experience.

docs.title: 6. Third-Party Services
docs.text:
Bedrock Health Group may use third-party services to support website functionality, analytics, hosting, security, or embedded content.

These third parties may use cookies or similar technologies according to their own policies.

docs.title: 7. Updates to this Policy
docs.text:
We may update this Cookies Policy from time to time to reflect changes in technology, legal requirements, or organizational practices.

Updates will be posted with a revised Effective Date when applicable.

docs.title: 8. Contact Us
docs.text:
For questions about this Cookies Policy, contact:

Bedrock Health Group – Privacy Office

Email: compliance@bedrockhealthgroup.org

docs.text: Version Date Summary of Change Reviewed By Approved By 1.0 Not specified in source website Initial publication from Bedrock Health Group website policy source. Privacy Office Governance & Compliance

docs.title: Email Security and Phishing Response Policy
Document Control
docs.text: Field Detail Policy Title Email Security and Phishing Response Policy Policy ID BHG-SEC-001 Version 1.0 Status Published Policy Area Privacy & Security Effective Date 2026-04-25 Last Reviewed 2026-04-25 Next Review Due 2027-04-25 Review Cycle Annual Policy Owner Privacy & Security Approved By Governance & Compliance Confidentiality Level Publicly Accessible Policy Source Bedrock Health Group policy library template and general cybersecurity awareness principles

docs.title: 1. Purpose
docs.text:
The purpose of this policy is to establish Bedrock Health Group's standards for recognizing, reporting, and responding to phishing, suspicious emails, malicious links, unsafe attachments, fraudulent messages, impersonation attempts, and related social engineering activity.

This policy is intended to reduce the risk of unauthorized access, credential theft, financial fraud, malware infection, privacy incidents, protected information exposure, reputational harm, and disruption to Bedrock Health Group systems.

docs.title: 2. Scope
docs.text:
This policy applies to all individuals who access or use Bedrock Health Group email, Microsoft 365, Outlook, Microsoft Teams, websites, portals, devices, shared files, collaboration tools, or related communication systems.

This includes employees, officers, trustees, contractors, volunteers, advisors, committee members, vendors, and other authorized users.

docs.title: 3. Policy Statement
docs.text:
Bedrock Health Group requires all covered users to exercise caution when receiving, opening, replying to, forwarding, or acting on emails, Teams messages, attachments, links, login prompts, payment requests, document-sharing notices, account alerts, or other electronic communications.

Covered users must not click suspicious links, open unexpected attachments, provide passwords, provide multifactor authentication codes, approve unexpected multifactor authentication prompts, send sensitive information, or complete financial or account-change requests without appropriate verification.

Suspected phishing, impersonation, credential theft, malware, payment fraud, or suspicious communication must be reported promptly through the approved reporting process.

docs.title: 4. Definitions
docs.text: Term Definition Phishing A deceptive attempt to obtain sensitive information, credentials, money, or access by pretending to be a legitimate person, organization, system, or service. Social Engineering Manipulation intended to cause a person to disclose information, click a link, approve access, send money, change payment instructions, or take another unsafe action. Suspicious Message An email, Teams message, text message, website prompt, shared-file notice, or other communication that appears unusual, deceptive, unexpected, unsafe, or inconsistent with normal business activity. Credential Theft The unauthorized collection or use of usernames, passwords, multifactor authentication codes, tokens, or other access credentials. Covered User Any person subject to this policy.

docs.title: 5. Phishing Warning Signs
docs.text:
Covered users must treat a message as suspicious when it includes one or more of the following indicators:

  • Urgent demands, threats, penalties, rewards, or pressure to act immediately.
  • First-time, unfamiliar, infrequent, or external senders.
  • Messages from known senders that seem unusual, pressured, poorly timed, or out of character.
  • Poor spelling, unusual grammar, awkward wording, unexpected formatting, or inconsistent branding.
  • Generic greetings instead of the recipient's name.
  • Sender domains that do not match the claimed organization.
  • Lookalike domains, misspellings, replaced characters, or suspicious sender addresses.
  • Outlook, Microsoft 365, or Teams warnings that the sender cannot be verified.
  • Unexpected links, attachments, shared files, invoices, DocuSign-style requests, payment instructions, password reset notices, or account alerts.
  • Requests for passwords, MFA codes, bank information, gift cards, wire transfers, protected information, confidential documents, or changes to payment details.
  • Links that do not match the visible text or expected destination when inspected.
  • Attachments that were not expected or do not match the sender's normal communication pattern.

docs.title: 6. Required User Response
docs.text:
If a covered user receives a suspicious email, Teams message, link, attachment, file-sharing notice, login prompt, or request, the user must:

  1. Stop and avoid clicking links or opening attachments.
  2. Do not reply to the suspicious message.
  3. Do not provide passwords, MFA codes, bank details, protected information, confidential information, or payment information.
  4. Verify the request using a separate trusted channel, such as a known phone number, official website, or previously established contact method.
  5. Report the message using the approved reporting process.
  6. Delete the message after reporting, unless instructed to preserve it by Privacy & Security, Governance & Compliance, or an authorized administrator.

docs.title: 7. Reporting Procedure
docs.text:
Covered users should report suspected phishing through the appropriate Microsoft reporting tool when available.

For Outlook, users should select the suspicious message and use:

Report > Report phishing

For Microsoft Teams, users should use the available message reporting function and select the security risk, spam, phishing, or malicious content option when available.

If Microsoft reporting tools are unavailable, the user must notify Bedrock Health Group's designated security or compliance contact.

Security and compliance concerns may be reported to:

Bedrock Health Group - Governance & Compliance

Email: compliance@bedrockhealthgroup.org

docs.title: 8. If a User Clicks a Suspicious Link or Provides Information
docs.text:
If a covered user believes they may have clicked a malicious link, opened a malicious attachment, entered credentials, approved an unexpected MFA prompt, shared sensitive information, or interacted with a suspicious website, the user must immediately:

  1. Stop using the suspicious site, message, or interaction.
  2. Write down key details while fresh, including time, sender, link, attachment name, website, system involved, and information shared.
  3. Change affected passwords immediately.
  4. Use unique passwords for each affected account.
  5. Confirm multifactor authentication is enabled where available.
  6. Notify Bedrock Health Group's designated security or compliance contact.
  7. Notify any affected financial institution, vendor, partner, or account provider when appropriate.
  8. Report financial fraud, identity theft, or criminal activity to the appropriate authority when warranted.
  9. Preserve relevant evidence if instructed by Privacy & Security, Governance & Compliance, legal counsel, or an authorized administrator.

docs.title: 9. Responsibilities
docs.text: Role Responsibility Covered Users Identify, avoid, verify, and report suspicious messages promptly. Privacy & Security Maintain this policy, support security awareness, review reported threats, and coordinate response. Governance & Compliance Oversee policy publication, compliance alignment, review workflow, and documentation practices. Managers / Supervisors Encourage prompt reporting and prohibit retaliation for good-faith reporting. IT / Microsoft 365 Administrator Support message review, message tracing, account protection, security configuration, and remediation where applicable. Authorized Reviewers Review suspected phishing or compromise reports and recommend corrective action when needed.

docs.title: 10. Technical Safeguards
docs.text:
Bedrock Health Group may use technical safeguards to reduce phishing and email security risk, including:

  • Multifactor authentication.
  • Strong password requirements.
  • Microsoft 365 security controls.
  • External sender warnings.
  • Anti-phishing and anti-malware protections.
  • Restricted administrative access.
  • Role-based access controls.
  • Audit logs and message tracing.
  • User training and awareness activities.
  • Incident response and escalation procedures.

docs.title: 11. Training and Awareness
docs.text:
Covered users may be required to complete email security, phishing awareness, cybersecurity, privacy, or incident response training.

Training may include examples of phishing indicators, suspicious links, unsafe attachments, impersonation attempts, external sender warnings, business email compromise, credential theft, payment fraud, and proper reporting steps.

docs.title: 12. Documentation and Recordkeeping
docs.text:
Bedrock Health Group may maintain records of reported phishing, suspected compromise, remediation steps, user training, access changes, investigations, and related corrective actions.

Records should be maintained in accordance with Bedrock Health Group's applicable retention, compliance, privacy, and security requirements.

docs.title: 13. Enforcement
docs.text:
Failure to follow this policy may result in corrective action, additional training, access restrictions, contract remedies, removal of system access, or other action appropriate to the circumstances.

Good-faith reporting of suspected phishing or security concerns is encouraged and must not result in retaliation.

docs.title: 14. Exceptions
docs.text:
Exceptions to this policy must be approved by Privacy & Security, Governance & Compliance, or another authorized Bedrock Health Group representative.

Exceptions must not create unreasonable risk to Bedrock Health Group systems, data, users, partners, or public trust.

docs.title: 15. Related Policies and Documents
docs.text:
• Privacy Policy
  • Cookies Policy
  • Incident Reporting Policy
  • Records Retention Policy
  • Acceptable Use Policy
  • HIPAA Privacy Policy, if applicable
  • HIPAA Security Policy, if applicable
  • Information Security Policy, if applicable

docs.title: Document Control
docs.text: Field Detail Policy Title Privacy Policy Policy ID BHG-PRIV-001 Version 1.0 Status Published Policy Area Privacy & Security Effective Date Not specified in source website Review Cycle Annual Policy Owner Privacy Office Approved By Governance & Compliance Confidentiality Level Publicly Accessible Policy Source https://bedrock-health-group.webdemotesting.online/policies/privacy/

docs.title: 1. Introduction
docs.text: Bedrock Health Group is a nonprofit dedicated to advancing healthcare compliance, risk management, and public health education. Protecting personal information is central to our mission. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, share, and safeguard information across our programs, websites, and technology platforms.

docs.title: 2. Information We Collect
docs.text:
We may collect the following categories of information:

  • Personal identifiers: Name, email address, phone number, organization, job title.
  • Account credentials: Usernames for Bedrock portals, Microsoft 365, or partner platforms.
  • Healthcare-related information: Only when necessary for compliance projects, such as HIPAA training, RADV audits, or risk scoring projects. We treat this as Protected Health Information (PHI) when applicable.
  • Technical data: IP address, browser type, device identifiers, usage logs.
  • Cookies and tracking technologies: See our Cookies Policy.
  • Training records: Course completion, certifications, and compliance tracking results.

docs.title: 3. How We Use Information
docs.text:
We use personal information to:

  • Deliver compliance and training services.
  • Maintain secure access to Bedrock systems.
  • Provide technical and administrative support.
  • Monitor program impact and generate reporting.
  • Conduct audits, risk scoring, and compliance assessments.
  • Meet regulatory requirements, including HIPAA, GDPR, HITECH, and other applicable requirements.
  • Improve our services and user experience.

docs.title: 4. Legal Basis for Processing
docs.text:
Depending on context, our use of data is based on:

  • Consent, such as signing up for newsletters or training enrollment.
  • Contractual necessity, such as delivering compliance services to clients.
  • Legal obligation, such as HIPAA or IRS requirements for nonprofits.
  • Legitimate interest, such as securing systems and monitoring service performance.

docs.title: 5. Sharing of Information
docs.text:
We do not sell personal information. We may share data with:

  • Authorized staff and contractors who must follow strict confidentiality.
  • Vendors and partners, such as IT hosting providers or RADV audit partners, under binding agreements.
  • Regulatory agencies only when legally required.
  • Emergency response vendors if engaged during a breach or compliance incident.

docs.title: 6. Data Retention
docs.text:
• Personal data is kept only as long as necessary for compliance, legal, and operational purposes.
  • Training and certification records are typically retained for one year unless client contracts specify otherwise.
  • PHI is retained and disposed of in compliance with HIPAA.
  • Technical logs are retained no longer than 12 months unless under investigation.

docs.title: 7. Security
docs.text:
We implement administrative, technical, and physical safeguards, including:

  • Encrypted storage and transmission of sensitive data.
  • Role-based access controls.
  • Regular audits and risk assessments.
  • Vendor security reviews.
  • Incident response and breach notification protocols.

docs.title: 8. Cookies and Tracking
docs.text: We use cookies and tracking technologies to support secure login, analytics, and user experience. Details are in our Cookies Policy.

docs.title: 9. Your Rights
docs.text:
Depending on your jurisdiction, you may have the right to:

  • Access and request a copy of your information.
  • Correct or update inaccurate information.
  • Request deletion, subject to legal and contractual limits.
  • Opt out of certain uses, such as marketing.
  • File a complaint with a regulator.

docs.title: 10. International Data Transfers
docs.text: If you are located outside the United States, your data may be transferred to the U.S. where our servers and vendors operate. We implement safeguards to protect transferred data.

docs.title: 11. Children's Privacy
docs.text: Our services are not directed to children under 13, and we do not knowingly collect information from them.

docs.title: 12. Updates to this Policy
docs.text: We may update this Privacy Policy to reflect legal, technical, or organizational changes. Updates will be posted with a revised Effective Date.

docs.title: 13. Contact Us
docs.text:
For privacy questions or requests, contact:

Bedrock Health Group – Privacy Office

Email: compliance@bedrockhealthgroup.org

docs.title: Revision History
docs.text: Version Date Summary of Change Reviewed By Approved By 1.0 Not specified in source website Initial publication from Bedrock Health Group website policy source. Privacy Office Governance & Compliance

docs.title: Approval and Publication Record
docs.text:
Approval evidence, review history, and publication history are maintained through Bedrock Health Group's controlled GitHub review workflow, board records, and applicable acknowledgement records.

This policy page does not serve as an individual signature or acknowledgement form.

Where individual acknowledgement is required, Bedrock Health Group may maintain separate acknowledgement records through a designated acknowledgement process.
